meta.notes: Each metric carries status/source. status 'proxy' = computed stand-in for a proprietary/unavailable series (see notes).

lei.notes: OECD Composite Leading Indicator, US (amplitude-adjusted) — current; replaces discontinued USSLIND
lei.decline_note: LEI declining 0 consecutive month(s) — threshold is 3

nfci_leverage.notes: Chicago Fed NFCI nonfinancial leverage subindex

lending_standards.notes: SLOOS: net % of banks tightening C&I loan standards — leads recessions 2-4 quarters

cc_delinquency.notes: Credit card delinquency rate, all commercial banks

core_pce.notes: Core PCE price index (Fed's preferred inflation gauge), index

ppi.notes: Producer Price Index, Final Demand (SA) — leads CPI by 1-3 months

philly_coincident.notes: Philly Fed Coincident Index — 4-factor current economic activity

sahm_rule.notes: Sahm Rule: 3mo avg unemployment rise from 12mo low. >=0.5 = recession onset

avg_hourly_earnings.notes: Average hourly earnings, total private (for real wage growth)

hy_oas.notes: ICE BofA US High-Yield option-adjusted credit spread
hy_oas.complacency_note: Spreads below 350bps indicate compressed risk premiums. This is not 'safe' — it means risk is being mispriced. The GFC peak (Oct 2007) saw HY OAS at only 270bps before spreads exploded to 1,900bps within 14 months.

ig_oas.notes: ICE BofA US Investment-Grade option-adjusted credit spread

equity_mktcap.notes: Fed B.103 market value of equities (Buffett numerator; Wilshire removed from FRED Jun 2024)

real_10y.notes: 10yr TIPS yield (real) — for Excess CAPE Yield

margin_debt.notes: FINRA margin debt (Fed Flow of Funds, quarterly)

recession_prob.source: derived: Estrella-Mishkin (NY Fed model) from T10Y3M
recession_prob.notes: 12-month-ahead recession probability. NY Fed Estrella-Mishkin probit model: P = Φ(-0.5333 - 0.6629 × 10y3m spread). Crossed 30% before every US recession since 1969 (one near-miss, 1998).

hy_momentum.notes: HY OAS 90-day rate-of-change converted to 0-100 risk score. >75 = spreads widening fast = credit stress signal.

lei_decline_streak.notes: LEI has declined for 0 consecutive months. 3+ months preceded every post-1950 US recession.

buffett_indicator.notes: Market value of US equities / GDP (Buffett indicator).

excess_cape_yield.notes: Excess CAPE Yield = CAPE earnings yield minus real 10yr.

ad_line_proxy.notes: RSP/SPY ratio as breadth divergence proxy. Falling = megacap masking.

pct_above_200dma.notes: percent of S&P 500 members above their 200-DMA

mcclellan.notes: Ratio-adjusted McClellan Oscillator (S&P500 proxy). Seeds/accumulates via state file across daily runs; trust after ~40 sessions.

hindenburg_omen_today.notes: PROXY on S&P500 (true indicator is NYSE-wide). RAW same-day flag — retained as breadth data only. High false positive rate; removed from alerts.

titanic_syndrome_today.notes: PROXY on S&P500. RAW same-day flag. Retained as breadth data only. High false positive rate; removed from alerts.

zweig_adv_ratio.notes: Advancers/(Adv+Dec). Zweig Breadth Thrust = 10-day avg moving <0.40 to >0.615 within 10 days; needs multi-day history (alert script).

hindenburg_omen_confirmed.notes: CONFIRMED when 2+ raw flags occur within 30 days. Retained as breadth data only — NOT an active alert (high false positive rate).

titanic_syndrome_confirmed.notes: CONFIRMED when 2+ raw flags occur within 30 days. Retained as breadth data only — NOT an active alert (high false positive rate).

fear_greed.notes: CNN's official Fear & Greed Index. 0=Extreme Fear, 100=Extreme Greed.
fear_greed.label: Fear

XLK.notes: Technology: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLK.name: Technology

XLF.notes: Financials: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLF.name: Financials

XLE.notes: Energy: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLE.name: Energy

XLV.notes: Health Care: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLV.name: Health Care

XLI.notes: Industrials: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLI.name: Industrials

XLB.notes: Materials: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLB.name: Materials

XLU.notes: Utilities: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLU.name: Utilities

XLP.notes: Consumer Staples: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLP.name: Consumer Staples

XLRE.notes: Real Estate: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLRE.name: Real Estate

XLY.notes: Consumer Discretionary: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLY.name: Consumer Discretionary

XLC.notes: Communication Services: 3-month relative strength vs SPY (pct pts)
XLC.name: Communication Services

composite.notes: 5-bucket weighted composite: credit(30%) cycle(25%) valuation(20%,capped70) breadth(15%) labor(10%)
composite.label: Moderate Risk

credit_score.notes: Credit bucket: SLOOS(40%) HY momentum(35%) NFCI leverage(25%)
credit_score.label: Elevated Risk

cycle_score.notes: Cycle bucket (v2): LEI direction(30%) YC risk(30%) Goldman composite(30%) Debt service(10%)
cycle_score.label: Moderate Risk

valuation_score.notes: Valuation bucket: CAPE(40%) Buffett(35%) ECY(25%). Capped at 70 in composite calculation.
valuation_score.label: High Risk

labor_score.notes: Labor bucket: Sahm Rule(50%) Unemployment trend(30%) CC delinquency percentile(20%)
labor_score.label: Low Risk

regime.label: No Signal
regime.context: 1 of 4 conditions active. Below historical watch threshold. Monitor — not yet actionable.
regime.threshold_note: 3+ conditions historically precede 20%+ drawdowns (excludes exogenous shocks: COVID, geopolitical events)

readings.cape: At the 94th percentile of all history; among the most expensive readings ever recorded — deeply stretched. Says little about this month, a lot about the next decade.
readings.buffett_indicator: Total market value vs GDP is at a record extreme versus its own trend (z = +2.5). Watch the deviation, not the level.
readings.excess_cape_yield: 0.06% — low — thin compensation for holding stocks over bonds.
readings.forward_pe: 22.0x next-12-month earnings — elevated, +6.0 vs the ~16x long-run mean.
readings.yield_curve_10y3m: Comfortably positive — no recession signal here; rising lately.
readings.hy_oas: Very tight — credit markets are complacent, little stress priced in.
readings.lei: Rising — leading indicators point to continued expansion.
readings.unemployment: 4.2% and falling — labor market still firm.
readings.core_pce: Core PCE running 3.4% YoY, above the Fed's 2% target and accelerating.
readings.core_cpi: Core CPI running 2.8% YoY, above the Fed's 2% target and accelerating.
readings.fed_funds: Policy rate 3.63%, currently on hold. Rate-cut cycles have preceded recent recessions.
readings.nfci_leverage: Negative — leverage/conditions looser than average (easy money).
readings.pct_above_200dma: 67% of the S&P 500 above its 200-DMA — broad participation — healthy.
readings.ad_line_proxy: Breadth confirms the index — no divergence.

watch: With valuations this stretched, favor quality and proven earnings over speculative names.
Breadth is broad — the tape supports staying invested.
Inflation is re-accelerating — watch the next CPI/PCE print and Fed tone.
interpretation.summary: Moderate Risk (50/100). Valuations are stretched — CAPE near historic highs, the Buffett indicator at a record, a thin equity-risk premium. Still, the cycle holds — leading indicators still rising, the yield curve still positive, credit spreads calm. Breadth is healthy, so no regime break yet.
